content.problemStatement:
Chef has been recently introduced to bitwise operations.

Chefina defines a function F(P,Q,R)=(RF(P,Q,R) = (RF(P,Q,R)=(R ∣|∣ P)−(QP) - (QP)−(Q &\&& P)P)P)

where ∣|∣ represents the bitwise or and &\&& represents the bitwise and operator.

Chef has three non-negative integers A,B,A,B,A,B, and CCC.

Chef has to count the number of integers XXX, such that:


0≤X<2C;0 \leq X \lt 2^C;0≤X<2C;
F(X,A,B)F(X,A,B)F(X,A,B) has the **maximum** possible value among all values of XXX.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing three space-separated integers A,B,C−A,B,C-A,B,C− as defined in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the count of XXX such that F(X,A,B)F(X,A,B)F(X,A,B) is maximum possible.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are 444 possible values of XXX which give maximum value for F(X,A,B)F(X, A, B)F(X,A,B). These values are 4,5,6,74,5,6,74,5,6,7.
For all these values, F(X,A,B)F(X,A,B)F(X,A,B) is 555.
Test case 222: The only possible value of XXX which gives maximum value for F(X,A,B)F(X, A, B)F(X,A,B) is 333.
For X=3X = 3X=3:

0≤X<220\le X \lt 2^20≤X<22
F(3,0,0)=(0F(3, 0, 0) = (0F(3,0,0)=(0 ∣|∣ 3)−(03) - (03)−(0 &\&& 3)=33) = 33)=3

It can be shown that F(X,0,0)F(X, 0, 0)F(X,0,0) cannot have a value greater than 333 for 0≤X<220\le X \lt 2^20≤X<22.
